title: carya ashei
intro: Carya ashei, also known as the Red Hickory or Ashe Hickory, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where the bark and leaves of the tree are used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Carya ashei include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its health benefits, including reducing pain and improving overall well-being.
